title: No explicit body size limit on webhook POST; Axum default allows 2MB JSON + unbounded media fetch
description:
src/server.rs builds the router (router()) with no DefaultBodyLimit layer and no RequestBodyLimit extractor on the /hook/{persona} route. Axum's built-in default is 2MB for JSON extraction, but:

1. An attacker can POST a 2MB JSON body containing up to 8 media URLs (max_media_per_post = 8). Each URL is fetched serially, each up to 10MB. Total upstream bandwidth consumed per webhook call: 2MB JSON + 80MB of media fetches = 82MB per authenticated request.

2. The 2MB axum default is undocumented behavior that can change between minor versions. No explicit cap is set by the application.

3. With valid credentials, a rapid sequence of webhook calls creates a sustained outbound bandwidth DoS amplified 41x.

Mitigation: add .layer(DefaultBodyLimit::max(65536)) to the /hook route and add explicit rate-limiting on authenticated webhook calls (not just inbox).
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
